element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players learn directional pad control for mid-air maneuvering
Some emeralds blocked by switches/bumpers requiring specific sequences
Each boss requires specific strategy; status strip provides hints
Three-ball rounds with tilt shake mechanic; over-use locks flippers
Life loss mechanic; directional pad allows recovery attempts mid-fall

typical_tension_triggers: Emeralds blocked by unmet switch conditions
Tilt shake overuse approaching flipper lockout
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Cyclical tension-relief structure: exploration phase (collect emeralds, navigate bumpers) → escalation phase (boss encounter) → relief phase (bonus round). Four-level progression creates macro rhythm.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Chaos Emeralds gated behind switch/bumper sequences signal optional paths within levels

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to flipper control and basic emerald collection; drains punish poor positioning
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Emerald gatekeeping increases; boss strategies become more complex
difficulty_curve.late_game: Fourth level boss and final emerald sequences require mastery of mid-air directional control and precise flipper timing
notable_spikes: Emeralds locked behind complex switch sequences (mid-game)
Final boss (escalating patterns; status strip guidance essential)

notable_level_patterns.description: Chaos Emeralds blocked by obstacles requiring players to hit specific switches or bumpers in order. Creates exploration and sequencing challenge within pinball mechanics.
Each level culminates with a boss encounter located at the top of the playfield. Boss-specific strategy required; status strip hints toward weakness patterns.
After boss defeat, player enters traditional pinball bonus round with three balls. Tilt mechanic adds risk/reward decision-making. Separate mechanical space from main level.
